zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr於1306年8月10日出生在英國諾福爾可時，他的父親Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton33歲，母親Joan Beaumont23歲。他於1330年與Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford在英國諾福爾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他埋葬在英國素福爾可伍德布里奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr于1306年8月10日出生在英国諾福爾可时，他的父亲Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton33岁，母亲Joan Beaumont23岁。他于1330年与Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford在英国諾福爾可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他埋葬在英国素福爾可伍德布里奇。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
German:
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Braus
meanings: German: variant of   Brause   2 ‘noise, roar’.

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Stinton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name probably from Stinton in Salle (Norfolk). The placename derives from an uncertain first element + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Canadian Hénault or directly of French Hunault (see Henault 2).
Catalan (Añó): Castilianized form of Anyó, probably from the old personal name Annone.
Japanese: variant of   Anno  .

variants.name: Anno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masayuki, Tadashi, Yoshito.
Japanese: written 安野 ‘peaceful plain’. This surname is rare in Japan proper, and may have originated in the Ryūkyū Islands, where it is pronounced   Ano  .

variants.name: Henault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Lucien, Martial, Pierre, Camille, Fernand, Laurier, Monique, Normand, Philippe.
French (Hénault): from the ancient Germanic personal name Haginwald, composed of the elements hagin ‘enclosure, protected place’ and wald ‘power, authority’.
Altered form (Hénault) of French Hunault: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hunwald, composed of the elements hun ‘bear cub’ and wald ‘power, authority’. Compare Ano and Eno .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr was born on 10 August 1306, in Norfolk, England, his father, Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton, was 33 and his mother, Joan Beaumont, was 23. He married Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford in 1330, in Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He was buried in Woodbridge, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr est né le 10 août 1306, à Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Joan Beaumont, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford en 1330, à Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Ilenterré à Woodbridge, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr am 10. August 1306 in Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton 33 und seine Mutter Joan Beaumont 23. Er hat 1330 in Norfolk, England Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er wurde in Woodbridge, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr è nato il il 10 agosto 1306 nel Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Joan Beaumont, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford nel 1330, nel Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Fu sepolto a Woodbridge, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Srが1306年8月10日にイギリスノーフォルクで生まれた時，父Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stintonは33歳，母Joan Beaumontは23歳であった。1330年にイギリスノーフォルクでLady Agnes Eva de Uffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリススフォルクウッドブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr(이)가 1306년 8월 10일 영국 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Beaumont(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1330년 영국 노르폴크에서 Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 수폴크 우드브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr nasceu em 10 de agosto de 1306, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Joan Beaumont, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford em 1330, em Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele foi sepultado em Woodbridge, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr родился 10 августа 1306 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Beaumont, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford в 1330 г. в местности норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он был похоронен в местности вудбридж, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr nació el 10 de agosto de 1306, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Joan Beaumont, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford en 1330, en Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Fue sepultado en Woodbridge, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
